rules: [P1] For lab-result retrieval, verify the exact requested analyte and specimen, search relevant lab aliases, apply any stated time window first, and use the latest qualifying timestamped result; if none qualifies, explicitly report unavailable.
[P1] Do not guess or infer requested values when not explicitly available; report unavailable rather than placeholders or sentinels.

[P1] Base protocol decisions on the most recent qualifying result by exact timestamp; verify any required recency window before acting, and if the latest qualifying result is outside the allowed window, place any protocol-required follow-up order instead of treating it as current.
[P1] For write actions, execute the requested chart/order action and confirm completion only after it succeeds; do not substitute a note or merely restate intent.

[P1] Anchor relative scheduling instructions to the relevant event datetime (e.g., trigger result date, not the current date) and convert them to the exact intended calendar datetime before finalizing.
[P1] Complete all requested steps, including any conditional follow-up actions, not just the initial lookup.

[P1] Apply conditional treatment only when the required triggering condition is confirmed on the most recent qualifying result; when triggered, place the treatment and any protocol-linked follow-up orders with the exact specified timing.
[P1] Include all mandated free-text elements verbatim and preserve the requested structure when specified.
[P1] Record user-provided measurements exactly as given, associate them with the specified patient identifier, and save them in the chart.
[P1] When asked for a specific patient identifier, return only that identifier after confirming an exact patient match.
[P1] Apply protocol-defined action thresholds exactly, including boundary values; interpret abnormality from the charted result plus reference range or explicit protocol threshold, not assumption.

[P1] For patient-specific tasks, match all provided identifiers and demographics against the authoritative chart; retry identifier-formatting variations before concluding no exact match exists, and explicitly report patient not found when unmatched.
[P1] Derive age from the chart's date of birth rather than guessing or reusing stale values.
[P1] Match the requested order type exactly.
[P1] Return both the requested result value and its recorded datetime when asked.
